qurbani_tips.title: Check health certificate
qurbani_tips.description: Always ask the seller for a valid health certificate issued by a registered vet before finalizing your booking.

qurbani_tips.title: Know the minimum weight
qurbani_tips.description: For goats and sheep, minimum weight should be 12 kg. Cows and bulls should be at least 150 kg to be valid for Qurbani.

qurbani_tips.title: Request live photos or video
qurbani_tips.description: Ask the seller for recent photos or a short video to verify the animal's actual condition before making any payment.

qurbani_tips.title: Pay deposit safely
qurbani_tips.description: Always pay via bKash or bank transfer. Avoid paying the full amount in advance to an unknown seller you have not verified.

qurbani_tips.title: Consider group Qurbani
qurbani_tips.description: One cow or camel can be shared among up to 7 people. Splitting the cost with family or neighbors is a practical and valid option.

qurbani_tips.title: Arrange delivery early
qurbani_tips.description: Book your delivery slot at least one week before Eid. Last-minute delivery requests often fail due to high demand in the city.

top_breeds.name: Friesian
top_breeds.note: Premium meat quality, large build, most popular for group Qurbani in Bangladesh

top_breeds.name: Shahibal
top_breeds.note: Most common deshi breed, widely available across all districts

top_breeds.name: Black Bengal
top_breeds.note: Most popular goat for individual Qurbani, naturally raised and disease resistant

top_breeds.name: Jamnapari
top_breeds.note: Large premium goat, excellent meat quality, ideal for families wanting a bigger animal

top_breeds.name: Merino
top_breeds.note: Calm temperament, grass fed, preferred by families who choose sheep over goat

top_breeds.name: Arabian
top_breeds.note: Rare and premium, valid for 7 family shares, full documentation and health certificate required
